verbGlossary.ACCEPTANCE-TESTED BY: {source} performs formal acceptance tests on installed equipment to confirm it meets contract performance specifications before owner turnover.
verbGlossary.APPROVED BY: {source} grants formal authorization for {target}'s design, procurement, or installation to proceed.
verbGlossary.BALANCED BY: {source} performs testing, adjusting, and balancing of air and water systems to achieve design flow rates and temperatures.
verbGlossary.BARRICADED BY: {source} establishes temporary fencing and perimeter protection to secure the site and maintain public safety during construction.
verbGlossary.BUDGETED BY: {target} establishes the cost baseline for {source} during preconstruction, allocating funds within the project budget.
verbGlossary.CAPACITY-CHECKED BY: {target} verifies that {source}'s installed capacity meets engineering calculations and code requirements.
verbGlossary.CERTIFIED BY: {source} performs final certification testing on installed systems and issues compliance documentation for occupancy or handover.
verbGlossary.CLASH-DETECTED BY: {source} runs 3D model interference checks to identify spatial conflicts between {target} and adjacent building systems before installation.
verbGlossary.CLOSE-OUT MANAGED BY: {source} oversees the final installation, warranty documentation, and owner acceptance of {target} during project closeout.
verbGlossary.COLOR-APPROVED BY: {source} reviews and approves final color selections, samples, and mockups for finish elements before full-scale application.
verbGlossary.COMMISSIONED BY: {source} performs functional performance testing of mechanical or electrical equipment to verify it operates per design intent.
verbGlossary.CONTRACTED BY: {target} executes the subcontract agreement and administers the commercial terms for {source}'s installation scope.
verbGlossary.COORDINATED BY: {source} aligns {target}'s installation with adjacent trades to prevent spatial conflicts and maintain construction sequence.
verbGlossary.DESIGNED BY: {source} develops the engineering design, calculations, and detail drawings for {target}.
verbGlossary.DRIVEN BY: {source} oversees pile driving or drilling operations for deep foundation elements, managing equipment and verifying bearing capacity.
verbGlossary.ENFORCED BY: {source} ensures {target}'s installation and maintenance complies with environmental or safety regulations through field monitoring.
verbGlossary.ESTIMATED BY: {target} performs quantity takeoffs and cost estimates for {source} during bidding or preconstruction.
verbGlossary.FALL-PROTECTED BY: {source} develops and enforces fall protection plans specific to elevated structural work, including tie-off points and guardrail systems.
verbGlossary.FINISH-SELECTED BY: {source} selects the specific material, color, and pattern for a specialty finish element based on design intent and project standards.
verbGlossary.FLOOD-TESTED BY: {source} conducts ponding or flood tests on completed membrane assemblies to verify watertightness before cover-up.
verbGlossary.FLOW-TESTED BY: {source} performs hydraulic flow tests on fire protection equipment to verify it meets NFPA flow and pressure requirements.
verbGlossary.FORMED BY: {source} constructs and sets formwork for cast-in-place concrete elements, establishing geometry and dimensions.
verbGlossary.HARDWARE-SCHEDULED BY: {source} maintains the hardware schedule that specifies locksets, hinges, closers, and accessories for each door in the project.
verbGlossary.HUNG BY: {source} installs overhead piping using hangers, supports, and seismic bracing per engineering specifications.
verbGlossary.INSPECTED BY: {target} performs quality control inspections on {source} at critical hold points during construction.
verbGlossary.INTEGRATED BY: {target} connects {source} into the building-wide automation system and verifies interoperability with other connected systems.
verbGlossary.LAID BY: {source} physically sets masonry units with mortar, maintaining coursing, alignment, and joint dimensions per drawings.
verbGlossary.LAID OUT BY: {source} transfers design dimensions to the field using survey instruments, establishing {target}'s position on site.
verbGlossary.LOAD-CALCULATED BY: {source} performs engineering load calculations to size equipment and verify it meets peak demand requirements.
verbGlossary.LOAD-TESTED BY: {source} conducts full-load performance tests on emergency power systems to verify they can sustain critical building loads during an outage.
verbGlossary.LOCKED OUT BY: {source} implements lockout/tagout procedures on energized electrical systems to protect workers during installation and maintenance.
verbGlossary.MODELED BY: {target} creates and maintains the 3D BIM model of {source}, including geometry, properties, and coordination data.
verbGlossary.PATTERNED BY: {source} develops tile layout patterns, grout joint lines, and cut plans to achieve the design intent for floor and wall tile installations.
verbGlossary.PENETRATION-COORDINATED BY: {source} coordinates all pipe and duct penetrations through structural elements in the 3D model to prevent conflicts during installation.
verbGlossary.PERMIT-PULLED BY: {source} obtains required permits and coordinates utility company inspections for regulated building systems.
verbGlossary.PLACED BY: {source} oversees concrete placement operations including delivery coordination, pour sequencing, and finishing for slab elements.
verbGlossary.PRESSURE-TESTED BY: {source} performs hydrostatic or pneumatic pressure tests on piping systems to verify joint integrity before concealment.
verbGlossary.PROCURED BY: {target} manages the procurement process for {source} including bidding, vendor selection, purchase orders, and delivery tracking.
verbGlossary.PROGRAMMED BY: {source} configures the control logic, addressing, and notification sequences for electronic building systems.
verbGlossary.PULLED BY: {source} installs electrical conductors through conduit and raceway systems to complete branch circuit wiring.
verbGlossary.PUNCH-LISTED BY: {source} identifies deficiencies and incomplete work on {target} during pre-final and final inspections, documenting items for correction.
verbGlossary.PUNCHED BY: {source} walks the completed element and identifies deficiencies requiring correction before the area is released for subsequent work.
verbGlossary.RIGGED BY: {source} plans and executes the crane lifts and rigging operations needed to set heavy precast or prefabricated elements.
verbGlossary.ROUTED BY: {source} determines the optimal 3D routing path for ductwork through the building, resolving conflicts with structure and other trades in the model.
verbGlossary.SCHEDULED BY: {source} assigns installation dates and durations for {target} within the project master schedule, establishing sequence and float.
verbGlossary.SCOPED BY: {source} defines the functional requirements and coverage areas for {target} based on owner operational and security needs.
verbGlossary.SELECTED BY: {source} chooses specific products, fixtures, or finishes for {target} based on design criteria and owner preferences.
verbGlossary.SEQUENCED BY: {source} determines the pour sequence and construction phasing for {target} across multiple floors or zones.
verbGlossary.SHOP-DRAWN BY: {source} manages the shop drawing process for fabricated elements, coordinating between manufacturer details and field dimensions.
verbGlossary.SIZED BY: {target} performs hydraulic or engineering calculations to determine the required diameter, capacity, or rating of {source}.
verbGlossary.SLEEVED BY: {source} installs pipe sleeves and penetration preparations through structural elements before concrete placement.
verbGlossary.SPECIFIED BY: {source} writes the technical specification section for {target}, defining materials, performance criteria, and installation standards.
verbGlossary.STAGED BY: {source} plans the material staging, traffic control, and phasing for large-area exterior work to maintain site access.
verbGlossary.STAKED OUT BY: {source} transfers wall layout lines from drawings to the floor slab using laser instruments, establishing framing locations.
verbGlossary.SUPERVISED BY: {target} directs the day-to-day field work for {source}'s installation, managing crews, equipment, and sequence.
verbGlossary.TESTED BY: {target} performs material testing (compaction, soil bearing, concrete strength) to verify {source} meets specification requirements.
verbGlossary.TORQUE-CHECKED BY: {source} verifies structural bolt tension using calibrated wrenches or tension indicators to confirm connections meet design loads.
verbGlossary.TRIMMED OUT BY: {source} installs the visible finish components of a plumbing or electrical system after wall and ceiling finishes are complete.
verbGlossary.UTILITY-COORDINATED BY: {source} manages the interface between the project and the utility provider, scheduling connections, inspections, and meter installations.
verbGlossary.VALUE-ENGINEERED BY: {target} evaluates alternative products or systems for {source} to reduce cost while maintaining performance during preconstruction.
